info.title: Core Server API
info.description: Reference for the server-side Core API.

region.description: Geographical region where your Nabla Core API organization exists.

post.description: Use this endpoint to get a medium-lived JWT access token for your backend to perform calls the Server API exposed by Nabla. See the [Authentication](/core-api/authentication) guide for details on how to construct the client assertion.

grant_type.description: This endpoint only supports the 'client_credentials' value.

client_assertion_type.description: This endpoint only supports the 'urn:ietf:params:oauth:client-assertion-type:jwt-bearer' value.

client_assertion.description: A short-lived JWT assertion that serves to authenticate this request.

200.description: A JWT access token that can be used to access the server API.

expires_in.description: The lifetime of the access token, in seconds.

post.description: Generate a transcript from an audio file. Only `audio/*` mime types are supported. The maximum duration is 10 minutes. If you have longer files, please use the [asynchronous equivalent](/api-reference/server/transcribe-async).

200.description: Results of processing the audio file.

post.description: Generate a transcript from an audio file. Only `audio/*` mime types are supported. The maximum duration is 60 minutes.

get.description: Poll the status —and results if successful— of an asynchronous transcription.

post.description: Generate a dictation from an audio file. Only `audio/*` mime types are supported. The maximum duration is 10 minutes. If you have longer files, please use the [asynchronous equivalent](/api-reference/server/dictate-async).

200.description: Results of processing the audio file.

post.description: Generate a dictation from an audio file. Only `audio/*` mime types are supported. The maximum duration is 60 minutes.

get.description: Poll the status —and results if successful— of an asynchronous dictation.

post.description: Generate a structured clinical note given the transcript.

Creates an asynchronous task (also called asynchronous request) that will generate a structured clinical note given the transcript, then keeps the response for a short period to be fetched using the poll endpoint.

get.description: Poll the status —and results if successful— of an asynchronous note generation.

post.summary: Generate normalized data from a note
post.description:
Generate normalized data from a Nabla-generated clinical note by extracting ICD-10 and LOINC codes in a FHIR-compliant structure.

It adeptly identifies and categorizes key medical information within the notes, such as past medical history, family medical history, clinical assessments, and vital signs.

Each piece of medical information is represented as an array of FHIR's conditions/observations, each including relevant ICD-10 codes, thereby ensuring compatibility with standardized medical data frameworks.

The Normalize functionality is tailored to enhance the integration of clinical notes into Electronic Health Records (EHRs), facilitating accurate and efficient documentation by aligning with FHIR protocols.

200.description: The normalized data extracted from the note.

post.description:
Generates clear, post-visit instructions based on what the healthcare provider communicated to the patient during the encounter. These instructions are intended to help patients better understand and follow their care plan.

:::note Provider review

Patient instructions are generated automatically and may be translated. A licensed healthcare provider should review and approve them before sharing with the patient, especially when translation is involved.

:::

This endpoint supports multiple input formats and languages to fit a variety of clinical workflows.
Edit the Nabla-generated clinical note following human-readable text instructions, to customize and have more control over its content and structure.
Create detailed feedback or report issues directly related to specific API interactions.

200.description: The created report id, to be referenced when emailing support.

external_id.description: A unique identifier for the user from another system which is given to Nabla.

get.description: Retrieve a list of your organization's Core API Users, ordered by creation time.
Get a single user by their id.

patch.description: Update a Nabla Core API user. Please be aware that this operation supports partial updates of the user's fields. However partial updates are not supported within the metadata dictionary. Therefore, to update a field within metadata, you must resend the entire dictionary each time.

external_id.description: A unique identifier for the user from another system which is given to Nabla.

parameters.name: external_id
parameters.description: A unique identifier for the user from another system.

get.summary: Find a user by their external id
get.description: Get a single user by their external id.

post.description: Deactivate a user. Deactivated users loose access to the API. They will face authentication errors and will not be able to use any feature.

Use this endpoint to get JWT tokens to authenticate a user with the Nabla Core API. These tokens only gives access to resources for the specified user. This endpoint should typically be called by your backend on behalf of an authenticated user, and the resulting JWT tokens should be forwarded to the user's frontend securely.

message.description: A human-readable explanation of the error, often including contextual details to help understand what went wrong.

code.description:
A numeric identifier for the error. This may either be a standard HTTP status code (e.g. 400, 401) or a more specific application-level code (typically >1000). Some errors —like an audio buffer overflow due to client misuse— have dedicated codes, while others —like malformed JSON— use broader and generic codes.

**Tip**: You should use this field as the primary reference for implementing error handling logic.

name.description: An internal enum-style name associated with the error code. While it may help with debugging, it’s mainly for reference—always rely on the numeric code for programmatic handling.

trace_id.description: A unique identifier for the failed API request. This is especially useful when reaching out to Nabla support, as it allows us to trace the exact request in our systems.

example.message: Unable to parse JSON at path $.speech_locales
example.name: BAD_REQUEST

example.message: Received a huge amount of audio in a very short window time.
example.name: AUDIO_CHUNKS_BUFFER_OVERFLOW

transcribe_config.description: First message to configure transcription (audio format, locale, etc).

streams.description: Describe the audio streams you intend to stream as input of the Transcribe API. Typically, if you have separate doctor/patient audio tracks (or do diarization yourself) you will configure two streams for transcription. Remember that a stream will expect audio to flow continuously, otherwise a timeout error will be thrown.

sample_rate.description: Sample rate of submitted streaming audio, in hertz.

speech_locales.description: An array of up to two locales for transcription. The speech-to-text engine automatically detects the spoken locale from this list. Each `transcript_item` received in the websocket will then specify the detected locale, enabling accurate transcription of **bilingual encounters**.

split_by_sentence.description: Indicates whether to segment transcription results at sentence boundaries. Default is false, meaning that a single transcript item may encompass multiple sentences, provided they are not delineated by pauses (silence) in the audio.

enable_audio_chunk_ack.description:
If enabled, audio chunks should contain a `seq_id`, and the server will periodically send acknowledgements up to the specified `seq_id`.

Read more in [Make transcription and dictation resilient to network interruptions](/core-api/best-practices/transcription-network-resilience).

payload.description: Raw audio chunk in base64 string.

stream_id.description: Identifier of one of the streams you defined in `streams` in the configuration.

seq_id.description:
A unique identifier for this audio chunk within its stream, expected to be sequential (n+1) from one audio chunk to the next.
First value (i.e. for the first audio chunk of a stream) can start at any arbitrary value.
This field is required if you enabled the audio chunk acknowledgement protocol in the configuration.

Read more in [Make transcription and dictation resilient to network interruptions](/core-api/best-practices/transcription-network-resilience).

transcribe_audio_chunk_ack.description:
Acknowledgement of audio receipt by the server up to the audio chunk with the sequential id `ack_id`.

Read more in [Make transcription and dictation resilient to network interruptions](/core-api/best-practices/transcription-network-resilience).

stream_id.description: ID of the stream to which the ack-ed chunk belongs.

ack_id.description: The sequential id of the audio chunk up to which this acknowledgment applies.

listen_config.description: First message to configure transcription (audio format, language, etc).

items.description: Specifies which items you want us to send you back. Currently only transcription (i.e. `transcript_item`) is supported.

streams.description: Describe the audio streams you intend to stream as input of the Listen API. Typically, if you have separate doctor/patient audio tracks (or do diarization yourself) you will configure two streams for transcription. Remember that a stream will expect audio to flow continuously, otherwise a timeout error will be thrown.

sample_rate.description: Sample rate of submitted streaming audio, in hertz.

language.description: Language spoken in the audio ('en' and 'fr' are still supported but deprecated, and respectively equivalent to 'en-US' and 'fr-FR').

split_by_sentence.description: Indicates whether to segment transcription results at sentence boundaries. Default is false, meaning that a single transcript item may encompass multiple sentences, provided they are not delineated by pauses (silence) in the audio.

enable_audio_chunk_ack.description:
If enabled, audio chunks should contain a `seq_id`, and the server will periodically send acknowledgements up to the specified `seq_id`.

Read more in [Make transcription and dictation resilient to network interruptions](/core-api/best-practices/transcription-network-resilience).

payload.description: Raw audio chunk in base64 string.

stream_id.description: Identifier of one of the streams you defined in `streams` in the configuration.

seq_id.description:
A unique identifier for this audio chunk within its stream, expected to be sequential (n+1) from one audio chunk to the next.
First value (i.e. for the first audio chunk of a stream) can start at any arbitrary value.
This field is required if you enabled the audio chunk acknowledgement protocol in the configuration.

Read more in [Make transcription and dictation resilient to network interruptions](/core-api/best-practices/transcription-network-resilience).

id.description: ID of the object you're receiving. It will be re-used as you receive more refined versions of this object, until you get the final version (the one for which `is_final` is `true`).

speaker.description: Who said the text in this transcript item. If no diarization, then this is simply inferred from the speakerType of the audio stream from which this sentence got transcribed.

start_offset_ms.description: The initial point of the audio segment that this transcript entry represents, measured in milliseconds from the start of the audio stream.

end_offset_ms.description:
The concluding point of the audio segment that this transcript entry represents, measured in milliseconds from the start of the audio stream.

In other words: this basically equals `start_time_ms` plus the duration of the audio segment that this transcript entry represents (regardless of how it was chunked).

is_final.description: Indicates if this is the final version of the transcript item.

stream_id.description: ID of the stream to which the ack-ed chunk belongs.

ack_id.description: The sequential id of the audio chunk up to which this acknowledgment applies.

example.message: Unable to parse JSON at path $.language

smart_dictate_config.description:
First message to configure dictation (audio format, locale, etc).
Unlike older WebSocket APIs, the Dictation API now consistently requires sequence ids in incoming audio chunks and  always emits audio chunk acknowledgment frames.
    
Read more in [Make transcription and dictation resilient to network interruptions](/guides/best-practices/transcription-network-resilience).

sample_rate.description: Sample rate of submitted streaming audio, in hertz.

payload.description: Raw audio chunk in base64 string.

seq_id.description:
A unique identifier for this audio chunk, expected to be sequential (n+1) from one audio chunk to the next.
First value can start at any arbitrary value.
Read more in [Make transcription and dictation resilient to network interruptions](/guides/best-practices/transcription-network-resilience).

smart_dictate_audio_chunk_ack.description:
Acknowledgement of audio receipt by the server up to the audio chunk with the sequential id `ack_id`.

Read more in [Make transcription and dictation resilient to network interruptions](/guides/best-practices/transcription-network-resilience).

ack_id.description: The sequential id of the audio chunk up to which this acknowledgment applies.

dictate_config.description: First message to configure dictation (audio format, locale, etc).

sample_rate.description: Sample rate of submitted streaming audio, in hertz.

dictate_punctuation.description:
When enabled, the speaker dictating would explicitly dictate the punctuation.
For instance, when the dictating provider says:

_The patient inquired **quotation mark** is this an allergy **question mark** **close quotation mark** displaying significant distress_.

the transcription would output:

`The patient inquired "is this an allergy?" displaying significant distress.`.

enable_audio_chunk_ack.description:
If enabled, audio chunks should contain a `seq_id`, and the server will periodically send acknowledgements up to the specified `seq_id`.

Read more in [Make transcription and dictation resilient to network interruptions](/core-api/best-practices/transcription-network-resilience).

payload.description: Raw audio chunk in base64 string.

seq_id.description:
A unique identifier for this audio chunk within its stream, expected to be sequential (n+1) from one audio chunk to the next.
First value (i.e. for the first audio chunk of a stream) can start at any arbitrary value.
This field is required if you enabled the audio chunk acknowledgement protocol in the configuration.

Read more in [Make transcription and dictation resilient to network interruptions](/core-api/best-practices/transcription-network-resilience).

dictate_audio_chunk_ack.description:
Acknowledgement of audio receipt by the server up to the audio chunk with the sequential id `ack_id`.

Read more in [Make transcription and dictation resilient to network interruptions](/core-api/best-practices/transcription-network-resilience).

ack_id.description: The sequential id of the audio chunk up to which this acknowledgment applies.

id.description: ID of the dictation_item you're receiving. It will be re-used as you receive more refined versions of this item, until you get the final version (the one for which `is_final` is `true`).

text.example: Patient showed signs of rapid improvement.

start_offset_ms.description: The initial point of the audio segment that this dictation entry represents, measured in milliseconds from the start of the audio stream.

end_offset_ms.description:
The concluding point of the audio segment that this dictation entry represents, measured in milliseconds from the start of the audio stream.

In other words: this basically equals `start_time_ms` plus the duration of the audio segment that this dictation entry represents (regardless of how it was chunked).

is_final.description: Indicates if this is the final version of the dictation item.

message.description: A human-readable explanation of the error, often including contextual details to help understand what went wrong.

code.description:
A numeric identifier for the error. This may either be a standard HTTP status code (e.g. 400, 401) or a more specific application-level code (typically >1000). Some errors —like an audio buffer overflow due to client misuse— have dedicated codes, while others —like malformed JSON— use broader and generic codes.

**Tip**: You should use this field as the primary reference for implementing error handling logic.

name.description: An internal enum-style name associated with the error code. While it may help with debugging, it’s mainly for reference—always rely on the numeric code for programmatic handling.

trace_id.description: A unique identifier for the failed API request. This is especially useful when reaching out to Nabla support, as it allows us to trace the exact request in our systems.

jwt_token.description: An authentication token using the JWT serialization format. JWT tokens contain varying data (depending on their intended use) and use a signature to guarantee their authenticity.

transcribe_request.description: The object containing all the information needed along with the audio file to transcribe.

speech_locales.description: An array of up to two locales for transcription. The speech-to-text engine automatically detects the spoken locale from this list.

split_by_sentence.description: Indicates whether to segment transcription results at sentence boundaries. Default is false, meaning that a single transcript item may encompass multiple sentences, provided they are not delineated by pauses (silence) in the audio.

speaker.description: Who said the text in this transcript item.

transcript_item.description: A portion of the transcribed consultation.

locale.description: Locale for this transcript item, detected by the speech-to-text engine from the list of locales in the input.

start_offset_ms.description: Start time of this transcription item as the offset, in milliseconds, from the start of the audio file.

end_offset_ms.description: End time of this transcription item as the offset, in milliseconds, from the start of the audio file. Equals the `start_time_ms` plus the duration of the related transcribed audio portion.

transcript.description: Transcript items from the audio file.

client_request_id.description:
An optional unique client-made id for this request. When specified, this id is included in  the Json payload that will be sent asynchronously. This can help clients both guarantee idempotency and correlate asynchronous responses with their requests.

_Note_: this id must be different for every request. It is recommended to use a randomly generated UUID.

transcribe_async_request.description: The object containing all the information needed along with the audio file to transcribe.

speech_locales.description: An array of up to two locales for transcription. The speech-to-text engine automatically detects the spoken locale from this list.

file_url.description: The URL of the audio file to download & transcribe.

split_by_sentence.description: Indicates whether to segment transcription results at sentence boundaries. Default is false, meaning that a single transcript item may encompass multiple sentences, provided they are not delineated by pauses (silence) in the audio.

id.description: The id of the asynchronous request.

error_message.example: We can't generate the note when the transcript is too short.

id.description: The id of the asynchronous request.
The id of the asynchronous request.

punctuation_mode.description:
Specifies how punctuation is handled. Possible values are:
• `EXPLICIT`: speaker must explicitly dictate punctuation (e.g. "comma" or "quotation mark").

dictate_request.description: The object containing all the information needed along with the audio file for which you want dictation.

dictate_async_request.description: The object containing all the information needed along with the audio file for which you want dictation.

file_url.description: The URL of the audio file to download & generate dictation.

id.description: The id of the asynchronous request.

transcript_item_request.description: A portion of the transcribed consultation.

note_template.description:
The desired template of the generated note.

Some templates are **only available in specific languages**. Check [Note template](/core-api/note-templates/note-templates-sections) for details.

note_section_key.description:
A key identifying a section of a note.
The set of possible keys depend on the template that is used. Check [Note template](/core-api/note-templates/note-templates-sections) for possible values.

note_section_customization_custom_instru.description: Allow to customize a note section by providing specific guidelines.  These guidelines must be as clear and concise as possible for best results.

note_section_customization_section_style.description:
Specifies the desired style for the note section:

  • `PARAGRAPH`: Prioritizes generating paragraphs;

  • `BULLET_POINTS`: Prioritizes structuring content using bullet points.

  • `AUTO`: Automatically picks the most natural formatting option.

Default is `AUTO`.

note_section_customization_level_of_deta.description: Specifies the desired level of details. Use `detailed` to get more details in the note section.

section_key.description: Key of the note section this customization should apply to.

split_by_problem.description: Flag to structure the content of the note sections according to distinct clinical problems or topics addressed during the consultation.

transcript_items.description:
The transcript from which to generate the note.

You don't need to specify each item's start or end time, but make sure they are sorted. In fact, due to its asynchronous nature, our [real-time transcription API](/api-reference/server/transcribe-ws) does not guarantee its results order. Thus, before calling this note-generation API please **filter-out non-final items and sort them by `start_offset_ms`**.

note_sections_customization.description:
Advanced per-section customization options for the chosen template.

You should **only specify sections that belong to the given template**, and **only specify customization options that are supported for the given section**. Also, only specify sections you want to customize, no need to be exhaustive.


Check [Note templates sections](/core-api/note-templates/note-customization) for compatibility matrix.

previous_note.description:
Optional initial Note to be enhanced using the new `transcript_items`.
This is useful if you generate notes gradually to avoid accumulating a very long transcript items list.
Typically, this will be the last note Nabla generated for you along with the new `transcript_items` that did not serve for its generation.

patient_context.description: Some contextual information that will be used to generate the note (for example name, gender, age, pronouns, medical history) [BETA].

sections.description: Content of the note structured in multiple sections.

example.title: Symptoms
example.text:
- Tiredness all day long
- Mild headaches on the right side

id.description: The id of the asynchronous request.

note.description: The Nabla-generated clinical note to normalize.

include_corresponding_note_problems.description: Indicates whether to include, for each condition, the title of the corresponding problem sub-header in the "Assessment & Plan" section of the input note. This field will only be returned when an `ASSESSMENT_AND_PLAN` section has been provided in the input note.

fhir_condition_clinical_status.description: The [clinical status](https://www.hl7.org/fhir/condition-definitions.html#Condition.clinicalStatus) of the condition. Only `ACTIVE` and `RESOLVED` are supported for now.

system.description: The used coding system, only ICD-10 is supported for now.

coding.description: ICD-10 coding representation of this condition.

corresponding_note_problem.description: The title of the problem sub-header corresponding to this condition, in the "Assessment & Plan" section of the input note. This field is only filled when `include_corresponding_note_problems` is set to `true` in the request and that an `ASSESSMENT_AND_PLAN` section has been provided in the input note. If the condition was not extracted from the `ASSESSMENT_AND_PLAN` section, this field will be `null`.

is_hcc.description: Indicates if the condition maps to a Hierarchical Condition Category (HCC) under the version 28 risk adjustment model.

is_mcc.description: Indicates if the condition qualifies as a Major Complication or Comorbidity (MCC).

coding.description: ICD-10 coding representation of this condition.

family_member_conditions_relationship.description: The relation to the patient of the family member the extracted conditions relate to, if applies.

conditions.description: Conditions of the family member of the patient.

system.description: The used coding system, only LOINC is supported for now.
The used coding system, only UCUM is supported for now.

low.description: The lower bound of the range.

high.description: The higher bound of the range.

coding.description: LOINC coding representation of this observation.

value_quantity.description: The value quantity for this observation, if not a range.

value_range.description: The value range for this observation, if not a quantity.

normalized_data.description: Normalized data extracted from the note.

conditions.description: FHIR conditions (including their ICD-10 code) corresponding to the patient's medical conditions as mentioned in the clinical note.

family_history.description: FHIR conditions (including their ICD-10 code) representing family medical history details extracted from the clinical note. Each element includes the relationship to the patient and the list of related conditions.

observations.description: FHIR observations (including their LOINC code) extracted from the clinical note.

patient_instructions_recipient_type.description: Recipient type of the patient instructions. This is especially useful for pediatric encounters.

generate_patient_instructions_request.description: The input to generate the patient instructions.

note.description: The Nabla-generated clinical note of the encounter.

instructions.description: List of instructions for the patient.
instructions.example:
- Order a sleep test that you can do at home. This will help us understand more about how you sleep.
- After you've done the sleep test, we'll talk about the results in our next meeting.

edit_note_with_instructions_request.description: The input to edit the note with instructions.

instructions.description: Human-readable text instructions for editing the note. Instructions should be short and straightforward, providing clear direction such as "Include discussion about…", "Make note more concise", or "Remove parts about…". The instructions should help refine and adapt the note to the desired format and content without requiring lengthy or complex prompts.
instructions.example: Add more details in the Plan section.

note.description: The Nabla-generated clinical note of the encounter.

transcript_items.description: The same transcript that served to generate the note. This transcript provides essential context, especially when instructions involve capturing more details or detailing specific parts of the encounter.

patient_context.description: The same patient context that served to generate the note, if any.

endpoint.description: The report is about a call to the [Generate Note](/api-reference/server/generate-note) endpoint (or its async version).

expected_output.description: The expected version of `output`. This is typically an improved version of the Nabla-generated note after it has been corrected or adjusted by the healthcare provider.

endpoint.description: The report is about a call to the [Generate Patient Instructions](/api-reference/server/copilot-generate-patient-instructions) endpoint.

expected_output.description: The expected version of `output`. This is typically an improved version of the Nabla-generated patient instructions after they have been corrected or adjusted by the healthcare provider.

conditions.description: FHIR conditions (including their ICD-10 code) corresponding to the patient's medical conditions as mentioned in the clinical note.

system.description: The used coding system, only ICD10 is supported for now.

family_history.description: FHIR conditions (including their ICD-10 code) representing family medical history details extracted from the clinical note. Each element includes the relationship to the patient and the list of related conditions.

conditions.description: Conditions of the family member of the patient.

system.description: The used coding system, only ICD10 is supported for now.

endpoint.description: The report is about a call to the [Generate Normalized Data](/api-reference/server/generate-normalized-data) endpoint.

expected_output.description: The expected version of `output`. This is typically an improved version of the Nabla-generated normalized data after it has been corrected or adjusted by the healthcare provider.

create_report_payload_request.description: Input and output of the related API call.

rating.description: Optional rating for the output of the given response: from very bad (1) to perfect (5).

feedback.description: Feedback on the given response. This should typically explain the rating, suggest specific improvements or highlight the good points.

reply_to.description: Optional email address for Nabla to contact for additional information or clarification regarding the submitted report feedback.

metadata.description:
You can use this parameter to attach key-value data to the object. You can specify up to 50 keys, with key names up to 40 characters long, all values must be of type string and up to 500 characters long.

You should **not** use this to store Protected Health Information (PHI) or any patient-related data.

created_at.description: The creation date of this object, in ISO 8601 format.

activated.description: Always true unless you explicitly [deactivate](/api-reference/server/deactivate-copilot-user) the user.

external_id.description: A unique identifier for the user from another system which is given to Nabla.

next_cursor.description: An opaque cursor to fetch the next page of the collection.

session_tokens.description: The tokens for an authenticated session.

access_token.description: A (typically) short-lived JWT token used to access authenticated resources, e.g. the Nabla User API.

refresh_token.description: A (typically) long-lived JWT token allowing you to "refresh" your access to resources (i.e. extend your session,  returning a fresh access token and a new refresh token with a later expiration).

id.description: Give an identifier to this stream.

speaker_type.description: Who is going to speak in this audio stream.

id.description: ID of the object you're receiving. It will be re-used as you receive more refined versions of this object, until you get the final version (the one for which `is_final` is `true`).

speaker_type.description: Who said the text in this transcript item. If no diarization, then this is simply inferred from the speaker type of the audio stream from which this sentence got transcribed.

locale.description: Locale for this transcript item, detected by the speech-to-text engine from the list of locales in the input.

start_offset_ms.description: The initial point of the audio segment that this transcript entry represents, measured in milliseconds from the start of the audio stream.

end_offset_ms.description:
The concluding point of the audio segment that this transcript entry represents, measured in milliseconds from the start of the audio stream.

In other words: this basically equals `start_time_ms` plus the duration of the audio segment that this transcript entry represents (regardless of how it was chunked).

is_final.description: Indicates if this is the final version of the transcript item.

legacy_speaker.description: Who said the text in this transcript item.

id.description: Give an identifier to this stream.

speaker_type.description: Who is going to speak in this audio stream.

legacy_language.description: Language of the transcript and the note ('fr' and 'en' are deprecated, and correspond to 'fr-FR' and 'en-US' respectively. Generating a note in 'es-ES' and 'es-MX' is not supported and will be soon removed).

smart_dictate_config_text_field_context.description:
Information about the text field in which the user is currently dictating. Having this information ensures that capitalization of the first word of the dictation is correct. It contains:
* **`text`** – the full, current content of the text field.   
* **`selection_start`** / **`selection_length`** – either  
  * the caret position where the dictation result will be **inserted** when nothing is selected (in that case `selection_start` is the cursor index and `selection_length = 0`).
  * the range of text currently selected and expected to be **replaced** by the dictation WebSocket output.

Validation rules (violations will close the WebSocket with code 1002 Protocol Error):
1. `0 ≤ selection_start ≤ text.length`  
2. Let `selection_end = selection_start + selection_length`. It must satisfy `selection_end ≤ text.length`.

text.description: Text content of the text field.

selection_start.description: Selection start of the cursor in the text field.

selection_length.description: Selection length of the cursor in the text field.

id.description: The unique identifier of this webhook event.

created_at.description: Creation date of this webhook event.

type.description: Type of this event, serves as a discriminator between the different possible webhook types.

allOf.description: Event sent when an **asynchronous** note generation succeeds.
Event sent when an **asynchronous** note generation fails.
Event sent when an **asynchronous** transcription succeeds.
Event sent when an **asynchronous** transcription fails.
Event sent when an **asynchronous** dictation succeeds.
Event sent when an **asynchronous** dictation fails.

- LDL cholesterol: 2 g/L

async_request_id.name: id
async_request_id.description: The id of the asynchronous request.

cursor.name: cursor
cursor.description: The cursor you received in the next_cursor field to fetch the next page.

limit.name: limit
limit.description: The amount of objects to retrieve, between 1 and 100.

order.name: order
order.description: This flag is used to get results sorted in ascending or descending order.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.description: The unique identifier of the user.
